diary: Monday started early and quiet—just me, coffee, and the sunrise from my kitchen window in West Seattle. The drive to First Hill was smooth, which felt lucky. The clinic was packed; I had back-to-back sessions with a runner dealing with IT band issues and a volleyball player rehabbing her shoulder. Around lunchtime, my coworker Maya and I grabbed sandwiches at Molly Moon's. She kept checking her phone for the World Cup match updates—apparently half the bars downtown were already buzzing even though it was only midday. I stayed focused on my afternoon appointments instead. After work, I needed to move, so I drove out to Discovery Park for a trail run. The weather was perfect—that golden early-June light filtering through the trees made everything feel lighter somehow. By the time I got home, showered off, and settled in with dinner, I was genuinely tired in a good way. Texted with friends for a bit, checked the ferry schedule out of habit (no trips planned), and called it a night. Days like this remind me why I love my routine.
thought_process: I was in a pretty grounded headspace today. Work felt purposeful—my patients are making real progress, and that keeps me motivated. I noticed the World Cup buzz around the city but didn't feel pulled into it; sports entertainment just isn't my thing, even when it's happening everywhere. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but I was aware of it affecting others. My mood definitely shifted once I hit the trail though—I needed that physical outlet after being in clinic mode all day. Running clears my head in a way nothing else does. I think I chose the trail run partly because the weather was too good to waste, but also because I was craving solitude after a day of being 'on' with patients. By evening I was content, tired, and ready to be still.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
